meta_title: For Students: content creation to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to content creation and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: content creation to Solve Debugging Roadblocks
content.seo_description: Learn how to use content creation to overcome debugging hurdles. A professional case study for students using Antigravity and Claude Code for SaaS development.
content.answer_block: Debugging isn't just about fixing syntax; it's about documenting your cognitive process. By creating content—specifically 'explain-your-code' logs—you externalize your logic, making errors visible. Use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to perform deep-dive analysis, turning every bug into a refined piece of technical documentation.
content.intro:
The cursor blinks in the void—a rhythmic reminder of the hours lost to a silent, cryptic error. You've been staring at the same function for three hours, and the frustration is mounting into a wall that feels insurmountable. Every student developer hits this wall; the difference between the hobbyist and the engineer is how they dismantle it.

Instead of spiraling, we treat debugging as a content creation exercise. By externalizing your code's intent through structured documentation and AI-assisted analysis, you stop fighting the compiler and start architecting the solution. This is how you transform technical debt into a portfolio piece while building your reputation as a developer who can ship.

steps.title: Externalize the Logic
steps.description: Before fixing, document the intended behavior. Writing out what the code *should* do exposes the gap between reality and expectation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Architect. Analyze the following code block. Break down the state transitions and the expected output for each logic branch to create a clear documentation map.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./src/logic.js --task 'Document the state machine and logic flow for this component.'

steps.title: Automated Trace Analysis
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a forensic trace of your execution stack, identifying exactly where the runtime environment deviates from your documented logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Given the documentation map, identify the specific line where the state transition fails during execution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --trace 'Find the variable mutation that causes the undefined error in the main loop.'

steps.title: Structural Refactoring
steps.description: Refactor the broken module into smaller, testable chunks. This reduces the surface area for bugs and makes the code self-documenting.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this monolithic function into three modular, pure functions. Ensure each function has a clear purpose and a single responsibility.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit ./src/logic.js --refactor 'Split monolith into modular functions with typed inputs.'

steps.title: Validation through Testing
steps.description: Create automated tests based on your initial documentation. If the code passes the tests, the bug is officially resolved.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a suite of Jest tests that cover the edge cases identified in the documentation map.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --test 'Create and execute unit tests for the refactored logic modules.'

steps.title: Publishing the Fix
steps.description: Turn your debugging session into a technical blog post. Explaining the 'why' and 'how' cements your knowledge and builds your professional brand.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Summarize the debugging process into a concise, technical post for my developer portfolio, highlighting the problem, the logical error, and the resolution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./src/post.md --finalize 'Draft a technical case study post based on the fix implemented.'

pro_tips: Treat every bug as a feature request for better documentation.
Use 'rubber-ducking' via AI prompts to force yourself to explain code line-by-line.
Never fix a bug without writing a corresponding test case to prevent regression.
Maintain a 'Bug Journal' to track recurring patterns in your own coding style.
Always refactor for readability first; optimization is a secondary concern.
content.next_steps: Now that you have automated your debugging workflow, apply this same 'Explain-Refactor-Test' loop to your next feature implementation to maintain high-quality code standards from the first line.
content.conversion_block: Stop struggling with cryptic errors and start shipping production-grade code with confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
